title: SMB Operations Core
summary: Reusable small-business primitives for scheduling, CRM, work orders, messaging, inventory, billing, and operations queues.

retrieval.description: Use when a requested product looks like an SMB operating system, service workflow, booking surface, CRM, WhatsApp inbox, repair flow, billing flow, or delivery/inventory app.

donorSources.label: Cal.com
donorSources.notes: Scheduling, availability, bookings, and calendar primitives.

donorSources.label: Chatwoot
donorSources.notes: Inbox, conversations, contacts, channels, and team assignment.

donorSources.label: Odoo
donorSources.notes: Appointments, repairs, POS, inventory, accounting, and subscriptions.

donorSources.label: ERPNext
donorSources.notes: Accounting, stock, selling, buying, support, and workflow primitives.

donorSources.label: Twenty CRM
donorSources.notes: Modern CRM object model and extensibility donor.

prompt: Model SMB apps through reusable primitives: Party, Resource, Request, Thread, Assignment, Policy, Transaction, Schedule, Reference, and LineItem.
Every workflow should have explicit lifecycle states and conflict handling.
UI screens must expose the next operational action, not just records.
Integrations must be treated as untrusted external systems with idempotency and reconciliation.
requiredElements: Canonical entity graph with relationships and lifecycle states
Conflict and idempotency policy for mutating workflows
Audit or history record for customer, billing, scheduling, and message mutations
Operational dashboard with pending, blocked, failed, and recently completed states
forbiddenPatterns: crud-only admin with no workflow states
deleting active business records without soft-delete or constraints
allowedSubstitutions: Use generic Party/Request/Transaction names internally when the vertical term is unclear.
Use soft-delete if hard relational constraints are not available.
Use mocked provider references during prototype generation, but keep provider reference fields in the model.
stackHints: Postgres with Drizzle or Kysely is the default for relational SMB state.
Use Hono or Fastify for API surfaces unless an enterprise framework is explicitly needed.
Use TanStack Query for frontend server state and optimistic workflow updates.

validators.description: Core workflows must have explicit allowed state transitions.
validators.evidence: Model/schema fields, service transition functions, or generated workflow tests.

validators.description: Billing/payment flows must preserve amounts, provider references, and reconciliation status.
validators.evidence: Invoice/payment schema, mutation tests, and audit history.

validators.description: Booking and delivery workflows must guard against overlapping or impossible time windows.
